## 🎯 Type of Change
<!-- Mark with an `x` all that apply -->
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation (changes to documentation)
- [ ] 🎨 Style (formatting, missing semicolons, etc; no code changes)
- [ ] ♻️ Refactoring (no functional changes, no API changes)
- [ ] ⚡ Performance (changes that improve performance)
- [ ] 🔧 Configuration (changes to configuration files)
- [ ] 🔒 Security (security improvements)

## 📋 Checklist
<!-- Mark with an `x` all completed items -->

### Code Quality
- [ ] My code follows the project's style guidelines
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [ ] I have commented my code, particularly in hard-to-understand areas
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] I have removed any debug/console statements

### Documentation
- [ ] I have updated the documentation accordingly
- [ ] I have updated the API documentation (if applicable)
- [ ] I have updated the README.md (if applicable)
- [ ] I have added/updated inline code comments

### Testing
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [ ] New and existing unit tests pass locally with my changes
- [ ] I have tested all user-facing changes
- [ ] I have tested error handling scenarios

### Security & Performance
- [ ] I have considered security implications of my changes
- [ ] I have not introduced any security vulnerabilities
- [ ] I have considered performance implications
- [ ] My changes do not degrade performance

### Database Changes (if applicable)
- [ ] Database migration scripts are included
- [ ] Migration scripts are tested
- [ ] Rollback plan is documented
- [ ] Database changes are backward compatible

### Dependencies
- [ ] I have not added unnecessary dependencies
- [ ] All new dependencies are documented
- [ ] Dependency versions are pinned appropriately
